source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Enable Encryption in Transit

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Enable Encryption in Transit
input_row.description: Amazon Relational Database uses SSL/TLS to encrypt data during transit. To secure your data in transit the individual should identify their client application and what is supported by SSL/TLS to configure it correctly.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console • Sign into the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon RDS Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/rds/. 3. Select the RDS Instance • Choose the Amazon RDS instance you want to implement encryption in transit. • Click on the instance name to access its details page. • In the instance details page, navigate to the Configuration or Encryption & Security section. 4. Enable SSL/TLS • Under the Connectivity or Encryption in Transit section • Click the Modify or Edit option to enable SSL/TLS encryption. • Select the option to enable SSL/TLS encryption. • Choose the SSL/TLS certificate authority (CA) certificate option that best suits your needs: o If you have an existing certificate, select Use a certificate from ACM (AWS Certificate Manager) or Use a certificate from AWS Secrets Manager. o If you do not have a certificate, select Generate a new certificate. Click Continue or Save to apply the changes.   5. Verify SSL/TLS Encryption • After enabling SSL/TLS encryption, monitor the encryption status of your RDS instance. • In the RDS console, check the Connectivity or "Encryption in Transit" section to ensure that SSL/TLS encryption is enabled, and the status is "In Progress" or "Enabled." 6. Test SSL/TLS Encryption • Connect to your RDS instance using a database client or application that supports SSL/TLS encryption. • Configure the client or application to use SSL/TLS encryption by specifying the SSL/TLS certificate details. • Verify that the connection is established successfully with SSL/TLS encryption. 7. Monitor and Manage SSL/TLS Certificates • Regularly monitor the SSL/TLS certificates associated with your RDS instances. • Manage certificate expiration and renewal to ensure uninterrupted SSL/TLS encryption.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Enable Encryption in Transit" for Amazon RDS instances can be automated because AWS APIs and boto3 SDK provide methods to check the SSL/TLS encryption status for RDS instances. This involves querying the RDS instance configuration to verify if SSL/TLS is enabled.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_rds_instance_encryption_in_transit_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods to use:**
  - Use the `boto3` library's `describe_db_instances()` method to fetch details about RDS instances.
  - Specifically, look for the `Endpoint` attribute in the response which includes `CertificateIdentifier` confirming SSL enabled.

- **AWS CLI commands (if applicable):**
  - Use `aws rds describe-db-instances` to retrieve RDS instance details.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Iterate over all RDS instances across all regions within an AWS account.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Verify that the `Endpoint.CertificateIdentifier` is non-null, indicating SSL/TLS is enabled.
  - Additional checks should ensure that the `VpcSecurityGroups` or `DBSecurityGroups` are set up correctly to allow secure connections.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass**: If `CertificateIdentifier` is specified for each RDS instance endpoint, indicating SSL/TLS encryption is enabled.
  - **Fail**: If `CertificateIdentifier` is not present or null, indicating SSL/TLS encryption is not enabled.

By automating this control, continuous monitoring and compliance verification can be seamlessly integrated with an organization's security posture management practices.
